tool_args.label: 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Preliminary Report Fall 2019

payload.title: 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Project Preliminary Report Fall 2019

payload.project_name: 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Project
payload.description: Pilot project implemented by NYCDOT in October 2019 aiming to improve operations of M14A/D Select Bus Service, increase safety on Vision Zero Priority Corridor, and maintain street as truck route

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.limits: 3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue (westbound) and 9th Avenue to 3rd Avenue (eastbound)

payload.description: TTP Pilot Project implemented on 14th Street
Painting and delineation of pedestrian space around Union Square
14th Street bus boarding platform installation
55 traffic enforcement agents deployed
16 traffic enforcement agents deployed
Preliminary Report released
New pedestrian space around Union Square
Painted curb extensions to shorten pedestrian crossings
Bus boarding platforms in the process of being installed as of November 2019

From 6 AM–10 PM, only buses, trucks, and emergency vehicles allowed on 14th Street from 3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue westbound, and 9th Avenue to 3rd Avenue eastbound. Other vehicles for local trips and garage access must make next available right turn. All left turns restricted except for MTA buses at certain intersections.
locations: 14th Street between 3rd Avenue and 8th Avenue westbound, and 9th Avenue and 3rd Avenue eastbound

payload.description: Sam Schwartz partnering with Traffic Databank for data collection
Sam Schwartz partnering with Public Works Partners for public engagement
14th Street TTP Pilot Project implemented by NYCDOT
Sam Schwartz monitoring the performance of the 14th Street TTP Pilot Project
14th Street is a Vision Zero Priority Corridor
M14 A/D SBS operates on the 14th Street TTP Corridor
TTP Pilot Project implemented October 3, 2019
Pedestrian space painting and delineation around Union Square
14th Street bus boarding platform installation

payload.gap_text: As a Vision Zero Priority Corridor, crash data will be reported in subsequent reports
payload.description: Crash data not included in this preliminary report; planned for future reports

payload.claim_text: M14 A/D SBS services started in July 2019
payload.description: M14 A/D Select Bus Service began in July 2019

payload.claim_text: 14th Street buses are more reliable, faster, and have higher ridership
payload.description: Summary headline for bus operations findings

payload.claim_text: Standard bus lanes are located on 14th Street from 1st Avenue to 3rd Avenue in both directions and 8th Avenue to 9th Avenue in the westbound direction. Additional bus priority treatments to be implemented in 2020.
payload.description: Existing standard bus lanes and planned additional treatments

payload.claim_text: The monitoring plan includes tracking and evaluation of MTA NYCT bus performance; vehicle speeds and volumes on area roadways; and bicycle and curbside activity
payload.description: Scope of monitoring plan

payload.claim_text: Varying changes in vehicle travel times on most east-west streets
payload.description: Vehicle travel time finding

payload.claim_text: Most north-south avenues experienced decreases in travel times
payload.description: North-south avenue travel time finding

payload.claim_text: Commercial vehicles occupy majority of curbside space
payload.description: Curbside activity headline finding

payload.claim_text: Citi Bike ridership in study area outpacing citywide growth
payload.description: Citi Bike finding

payload.claim_text: Vision Zero Priority Intersections on the 14th Street TTP Corridor include: 9th Ave & 14th St, 8th Ave & 14th St, 7th Ave & 14th St, 6th Ave & 14th St, 5th Ave & 14th St, 5th Ave & University Pl, 3rd Ave & 14th St, 2nd Ave & 14th St, and 1st Ave & 14th St
payload.description: Vision Zero Priority Intersections identified on map
MTA NYCT provided bus performance data
INRIX provided vehicle travel time and speed data
NYC DOT provided traffic count and curbside data
Citi Bike provided bikeshare ridership data
